markdown:
[Paul Sawers](https://venturebeat.com/author/psawers)
January 4, 2022


![Human Security (formerly White Ops) founders Michael Tiffany & Tamer Hassan](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Folk2kzr7lpbpdtkx.public.blob.vercel-storage.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2020%2F12%2FWhiteOps.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=85)

[Add to Google Preferred Source](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=venturebeat.com "Add to Google Preferred Source")

[Human Security](https://www.humansecurity.com/), a bot mitigation and fraud detection company for enterprises, [has raised](https://www.humansecurity.com/newsroom/human-raises-100-million-in-growth-funding-round-led-by-westcap-and-nightdragon) $100 million in a growth round of funding led by WestCap.

Bots have become more sophisticated through the years, adapting their behaviors to be more like people -- this can lead to so-called "click fraud," for example, whereby bots automatically tap on pay-per-click (PPC) ads to increase revenue for a website.

## Rise of the bots

Human Security, which [rebranded](https://www.humansecurity.com/newsroom/white-ops-reintroduces-itself-as-human-signaling-cybersecurity-companys-dedication-to-protecting-enterprises-from-bot-attacks) from White Ops back in March, offers various products designed to protect applications, advertisements, and marketing investments from such abuse, using machine learning and other threat intelligence techniques to identity malicious bots that mimic human behavior. Recent [data suggests that 73% of enterprises](https://venturebeat.com/2021/12/07/report-enterprises-lose-millions-of-dollars-to-ad-fraud-each-year/) lose 4% of their online revenue to ad fraud each year and, accordingly, Human Security said it has seen its revenue growth rate double over the past year as companies struggle to thwart automated attacks.

"The bot problem is one of the most important cybersecurity problems of modern times and verifying humanity is fundamental to the security and integrity of the internet as we know it," Human Security CEO and cofounder Tamer Hassan noted in a [blog post](https://www.humansecurity.com/blog/our-100m-growth-investment-round). "Botnets have become one of the most prolific modern attack tools -- our mantra is simple - stop the bots, stop the threats."

Founded out of New York in 2012, Human Security [was acquired back in 2020](https://venturebeat.com/2020/12/24/goldman-sachs-leads-acquisition-of-bot-mitigation-company-white-ops/) by Goldman Sachs’ merchant banking division, in partnership with ClearSky Security and NightDragon. Its latest cash injection included follow-on investments from NightDragon among other existing backers.

## More

[![PC infrared view of falcon](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F2quYmnQbaKi0HN803C5lQ9%2F447a0fb2036c755d927cf1ac60ae7e04%2FThermal_falcon_analysis_in_progress.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with OpenAI GPT-Image-1.5](https://venturebeat.com/security/ai-agents-that-automatically-prevent-detect-and-fix-software-issues-are-here)

By grounding AI in real-time enterprise context rather than just large language model reasoning, the company aims to move site reliability engineering and devops teams from a reactive posture to a predictive one.

[Carl Franzen](https://venturebeat.com/author/carlfranzen)
April 6, 2026


[![AdobeStock 1242306672 Preview](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F6kHmwEzYZ9i1FUJL3Icmp2%2F28157ad52046588fb3ae88e14b37a5fe%2FAdobeStock_1242306672.jpeg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)](https://venturebeat.com/security/closing-the-data-security-maturity-gap-embedding-protection-into-enterprise)

Partner Content

_Presented by Capital One_

[Andrew Seaton, Capital One](https://venturebeat.com/author/andrew-seaton-capital-one)
April 6, 2026


[![OCSF](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F1hnK3p6KoPnJhRtzL44Xab%2F1492dae9e6bd482ddd3ac2e9c5b4c2f1%2FOCSF.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
CleoP made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/security/ocsf-explained-the-shared-data-language-security-teams-have-been-missing)

The security industry has spent the last year talking about models, copilots, and agents, but a quieter shift is happening one layer below all of that: Vendors are lining up around a shared way to describe security data. The Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework _(_ OCSF), is emerging as one of the strongest candidates for that job.

[Nikhil Mungel](https://venturebeat.com/author/nikhil-mungel)
April 5, 2026


[![512,000 lines of exposed source code, three mapped attack paths, and the audit every enterprise running AI coding agents needs now](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F42hVjVx59CuomoskYro22S%2Fca222d6334907291406df1c9f24b713e%2Fcluade_code_hero.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
VentureBeat created with Imagen](https://venturebeat.com/security/claude-code-512000-line-source-leak-attack-paths-audit-security-leaders)

512,000 lines of source code. Three mapped attack paths. One six-layer audit matrix. What security leaders running AI coding agents need to do before Monday.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
April 2, 2026


[![Hackers slipped a trojan into the code library behind most of the internet. Your team is probably affected](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2FuyrfLkDZ8g0lgackcQVnS%2Fe0cb12e1e7988749e4ea84d1e77a9393%2Fhero_axios.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
VentureBeat created with Imagen](https://venturebeat.com/security/axios-npm-supply-chain-attack-rat-maintainer-token-2026)

Stolen npm token publishes cross-platform RAT through both axios release branches despite OIDC and SLSA. Third major npm compromise in seven months.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
April 1, 2026


[![Everyone told you to deploy AI agents. No one told you what happens to your SOC when you do](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F1zayI36Rne3LGkCx5LFR1w%2F5b777b759f1e5f19cac0e4d7ee377326%2FGeorge_Kurtz_by_venturebeat_.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Source" VentureBeat](https://venturebeat.com/security/rsac-2026-agentic-soc-agent-telemetry-security-gap)

The breakout window is down to 27 seconds. AI agents look identical to humans in your logs. And no vendor at RSAC shipped a fix for either problem.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
April 1, 2026


[![OpenClaw is already inside your enterprise and there is no native kill switch](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F69DOQfhXlWgAm6BEb5yRWx%2Fedd7628a720eecce0fbb34bb70e3a553%2Fhero_openclaw_story_March_2026.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
VentureBeat created with Imagen](https://venturebeat.com/security/openclaw-500000-instances-no-enterprise-kill-switch)

Three CVEs, 30,000+ exposed instances, and a CEO's data sold on BreachForums. The OpenClaw threat surface is bigger than anyone mapped.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
March 31, 2026


[![Five RSAC 2026 agent identity frameworks. Three gaps all of them miss](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F6fV5wwoyrhjNtVlzfCp3qY%2F93bce937ebe54e27dcfd010b6cb8a8c3%2Frsac_hero_.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: RSAC](https://venturebeat.com/security/rsac-2026-agent-identity-frameworks-three-gaps)

A CEO's AI agent rewrote the company's security policy. Every identity check passed. Five vendors at RSAC 2026 missed it.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
March 30, 2026


[![A Rogue AI Agent Just Breached Meta’s Internal Data Controls. The Confused Deputy Is the Identity Threat IAM Was Never Built to See.](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F6mU6gDy1sRfRCuDpkbhsK7%2Fb5010735714e37a63de5edf000917340%2Fhero_meta_story.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
VentureBeat created with Imagen](https://venturebeat.com/security/meta-rogue-ai-agent-confused-deputy-iam-identity-governance-matrix)

Post-authentication agent control doesn't exist in most enterprise stacks. Here's the governance matrix security leaders need before RSAC.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
March 19, 2026


[![VB1P1343](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F1nNxU4lCePcHgXl7pib5sq%2Fb7448e6ae3c654ac267280113a2609fd%2FVB1P1343.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Alex Stamos, chief product officer at Corridor, and Nancy Wang, CTO at 1Password](https://venturebeat.com/security/the-authorization-problem-that-could-break-enterprise-ai)

When an AI agent needs to log into your CRM, pull records from your database, and send an email on your behalf, whose identity is it using? And what happens when no one knows the answer? Alex Stamos, chief product officer at Corridor, and Nancy Wang, CTO at 1Password joined the VB AI Impact Salon Series to dig into the new identity framework challenges that come along with the benefits of agentic AI.

[VB Staff](https://venturebeat.com/author/vb-staff)
March 17, 2026


[![Five vendors, one Stack, zero precedent: The security architecture that shipped with Nvidia's agentic AI platform](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F5BTW8MTg00W3U0ZK7EWOti%2F6160c37c0124f3cdfe96f96898cd31fa%2Fcrwodstirke_nvidai_hero.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
VentureBeat made with Imagen](https://venturebeat.com/security/nvidia-gtc-2026-agentic-ai-security-five-vendor-governance-framework)

A five-vendor governance framework maps who covers what in Nvidia's agentic AI stack — and names three holes nobody closed.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
March 17, 2026


[![3 OpenClaw attack surfaces your security stack can’t see — and how to close them](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F3sE0om3WvLXwRMq2gTeMIh%2F3c6da20ea53298867e5c04df10f57030%2Fhero_openclaw_story.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
VentureBeat created with Imagen](https://venturebeat.com/security/openclaw-can-bypass-your-edr-dlp-and-iam-without-triggering-a-single-alert)

30,000 exposed instances, 824 malicious skills and three security gaps no current tool can close. Here is what your team needs to do now.

[Louis Columbus](https://venturebeat.com/author/louis-columbus)
March 16, 2026
